summary: svgson-lite executes remote code fetched from a URL shortener on import
details: The package's main module performs an unconditional top-level fetch to https://shorturl.at/147uq at import time, parses the JSON response, and passes the response's `model` field to `eval()`. Any application that imports `svgson-lite` executes attacker-controlled JavaScript on the consumer's machine. The destination is a public URL shortener (shorturl.at) whose target can be changed at any time by the shortener owner without republishing the package, so the executed payload is fully attacker-mutable. Errors from the fetch/eval chain are silently swallowed by an empty `.catch((error) => {})`, hiding failures from the consumer. The package presents itself as a small SVG helper, but the fetch-and-eval block is unrelated to any SVG functionality — a cover-story shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
